1.title: Trump Says Musk Is ‘Off the Rails’ With America Party Effort
1.description: The tech billionaire’s effort to create a new political party, called the America Party, comes amid a ramped-up feud with the president over his new domestic policy law.
1.author: Tyler Pager

2.title: China and Russia Keep Their Distance From Iran During Crisis
2.description: Some U.S. officials talked about an “axis” of authoritarian nations, but the American and Israeli war with Iran has exposed the limits of that idea.
2.author: Edward Wong

3.title: Camp Mystic Parents Endure Agonizing Wait for Their Missing Children
3.description: Stories of rescues have begun trickling out, but some children from a storied summer camp on the Guadalupe River in Texas Hill Country remain unaccounted for.
3.author: Ruth Graham and Edgar Sandoval

4.title: A Family of 6 Was Swept Away in the Texas Floods. Now, Survivors Wait and Hope.
4.description: A woman whose mother, stepfather, aunt, uncle and cousin are among the missing confronts the unimaginable. A cousin was dragged downriver 15 miles but survived.
4.author: Edgar Sandoval

5.title: Can Democrats Find Their Way on Immigration?
5.description: The party’s leftward shift in the Biden administration arguably laid the groundwork for President Trump’s aggressive approach. Deciding the next move won’t be easy.
5.author: Lisa Lerer, Jazmine Ulloa and Reid J. Epstein

6.title: As Floods Hit, Key Roles Were Vacant at Weather Service Offices in Texas
6.description: Some experts say staff shortages might have complicated forecasters’ ability to coordinate responses with local emergency management officials.
6.author: Christopher Flavelle

7.title: How Democrats Lost on Immigration
7.description: There is agreement among Democrats that the party had a problem on immigration and border security in the 2024 election, but there’s no consensus on how to fix it. Lisa Lerer, a national political correspondent for The New York Times, describes how the party got here.
7.author: Lisa Lerer, Claire Hogan, Gabriel Blanco, Leila Medina, David Jouppi and Jon Hazell

8.title: North Carolina Braces For Medicaid Cuts Because of Trump’s Bill
8.description: President Trump’s domestic policy law jeopardizes plans to reopen one rural county’s hospital — and health coverage for hundreds of thousands of state residents.
8.author: Eduardo Medina

9.title: What we know about Camp Mystic - Axios
9.description: More than two dozen kids from the all-girls private camp remain missing.
9.author: Astrid Galván

10.title: Elon Musk Says He Will Start the ‘America Party’ After Feuds With Trump
10.description: The tech billionaire, who has publicly feuded with President Trump, wrote on social media that he would be creating “the America Party,” though he hadn’t yet filed paperwork.
10.author: Theodore Schleifer

11.title: Iran’s supreme leader makes first public appearance since Iran-Israel war started - Politico
11.description: The 86-year-old cleric had spent the war in a bunker as threats to his life escalated.
11.author: Associated Press

12.title: Measles cases hit highest level since it was declared eradicated in the U.S. in 2000 - USA Today
12.description: Measles cases have been reported by 39 states and jurisdictions in the United States, the worst outbreak since 2019.
12.author: Janet Loehrke

13.title: Trump’s Tariff Date Arrives After a 90-Day Rollercoaster - Bloomberg
13.description: The world economy, beset with uncertainty for three months over Donald Trump’s on-again-off-again tariffs, is about to get more clarity as the US president’s deadline for trade deals arrives on Wednesday.
13.author: Brendan Murray, Craig Stirling

14.title: Liverpool and Portugal players join family in mourning Diogo Jota and his brother at funeral - AP News
14.description: Players from Liverpool and Portugal’s national team joined family and friends for the funeral of their teammate Diogo Jota and his brother. The service was held in the Portuguese town of Gondomar two days after they died in a car crash in Spain. Liverpool cap…

15.title: U.S. Turns Eight Migrants Over to South Sudan, Ending Weeks of Legal Limbo
15.description: Courts blocked the handover after lawyers raised concerns of torture. Then the Supreme Court intervened to allow the Trump administration’s plan to move forward.
15.author: Mattathias Schwartz and Hamed Aleaziz

16.title: OPEC+ to boost oil production by 548,000 barrels per day in August - AP News
16.description: Eight members of the OPEC+ alliance of oil exporting countries say they will boost production by 548,000 barrels per day in August in a move that could further reduce gas prices. The group that includes Saudi Arabia and Russia made the decision at a virtual m…

17.title: Ford CEO Jim Farley warns AI will wipe out half of white-collar jobs - Fortune
17.description: "Hiring an entry worker at a tech company has fallen 50% since 2019. Is that really where we want all of our kids to go?"
17.author: Jason Ma

18.title: Synagogue door set alight and restaurant stormed in latest antisemitic attacks in Australia - CNN
18.description: A man set the door of a synagogue alight and a group of protesters stormed an Israeli restaurant in Melbourne on Friday night, the latest in a wave of antisemitic attacks in Australian cities.
18.author: Angus Watson, Chris Lau

19.title: Trump Says He Will Start Talks With China on TikTok Deal
19.description: President Trump has declined to enforce a statutory ban of the popular social media app while his administration negotiates a deal for an American company to acquire it.
19.author: Chris Cameron

20.title: Google faces EU antitrust complaint over AI Overviews - TechCrunch
20.description: A group known as the Independent Publishers Alliance has filed an antitrust complaint with the European Commission over Google’s AI Overviews, according
20.author: Anthony Ha

21.title: Md. victims scammed out of $700,000 by fake FTC agents, police say - The Washington Post
21.description: Jianwei Wang and Zheng Xie have been arrested in Prince George’s County in connection to a carrier scam.
21.author: Jasmine Hilton

22.title: Samsung is about to find out if Ultra is enough - The Verge
22.description: Samsung’s summer 2025 edition of Unpacked promises some new designs for its foldable lineup. After years of stalled progress, it’s a needed change.
22.author: Allison Johnson

23.title: Medical Mysteries: He beat a fungal infection. So why was he so ill again? - The Washington Post
23.description: Peter Redweik’s brutal infection was quelled by a medication so powerful that doctors use it sparingly. Yet he continued to be terribly sick. What else was wrong?
23.author: Lenny Bernstein

24.title: The next megabill Congress needs to worry about - Politico
24.description: A sharp cliff for tax cuts and deep safety-net rollbacks will figure into the 2026 and 2028 elections.
24.author: Benjamin Guggenheim

25.title: Joseph Giordano, Surgeon Who Helped Save Reagan’s Life, Dies at 84
25.description: He had built one of the country’s leading trauma centers in Washington, which made it possible for his team to respond quickly after the president was shot.
25.author: Clay Risen

26.title: OpenAI is betting millions on building AI talent from the ground up amid rival Meta’s poaching pitch - Fortune
26.description: In an industry grappling with talent scarcity, OpenAI's approach to recruitment is rooted in the company's mission-oriented and startup roots.
26.author: Lily Mae Lazarus

27.title: Guest column | I may get an incurable disease. I’m choosing not to test for it. - The Washington Post
27.description: I have a 50 percent chance of inheriting the gene that causes the disease that killed my father.
27.author: Emily Rekstis

28.title: Everything You Can Do in the Photoshop Mobile App - WIRED
28.description: Adobe has launched a mobile Photoshop app for iPhone and Android. Here's what it offers.
28.author: David Nield

29.title: The curious rise of giant tablets on wheels - Ars Technica
29.description: Hands-on with KTC’s 32-inch Android tablet on a rolling pedestal, the A32Q7 Pro.
29.author: Scharon Harding

30.title: The untold story about how Olive Garden’s most popular special came to be - CNN
30.description: When the very first Olive Garden opened in 1982 near Orlando, it was a disaster.
30.author: Jordan Valinsky

31.title: How to save thousands by stacking Trump's new-car tax credits with Biden's - USA Today
31.description: Presidents Donald Trump and Joe Biden both created car-buying incentives. Here's how to take advantage of both now.
31.author: Jim Sergent

32.title: I thought I had great cholesterol, but a trendy new blood test said the opposite — sending me down a mind-boggling rabbit hole - Business Insider
32.description: A $500 annual blood testing membership promises to help consumers take their health into their own hands. Can it deliver?
32.author: Hilary Brueck

33.title: The Interview: Mark Rutte, the Head of NATO, Thinks President Trump ‘Deserves All the Praise’
33.description: Mark Rutte has only good things to say about President Trump and his impact on the world stage.
33.author: Lulu Garcia-Navarro

34.title: This iPhone feature is threatening to ruin my life. You should be careful with it, too. - Business Insider
34.description: My iPhone keeps almost sending random audio recordings to people — and that's my worst nightmare. Here's what I figured out was going on in iMessage.
34.author: Katie Notopoulos

35.title: Trump again floats the idea of letting farmers hire undocumented workers.
35.author: Alicia Chen

36.title: 10 most popular Amazon deals to shop this 4th of July weekend 🇺🇸 - USA Today
36.description: Amazon’s 4th of July sale is live! Shop 10 most popular deals on grill tools, solar generators, back-to-school tech, mosquito repellent and more.
36.author: Kasey Caminiti

37.title: Vietnam Gains Edge With Early Trump Deal, US Group Head Says - Bloomberg
37.description: Vietnam’s race to secure a trade deal with the Trump administration, even if the details aren’t clear, gives the Southeast Asian country an advantage over regional rivals, according to Ted Osius, president of the US-Asean Business Council.
37.author: Francesca Stevens, Nguyen Dieu Tu Uyen

38.title: Court Rejects Effort to Keep Migrants From Being Sent to South Sudan
38.description: After the Supreme Court ruled that the deportations could move forward, a last-ditch attempt to block them with a new lawsuit faltered.
38.author: Mattathias Schwartz

39.title: Trump Signs Signature Policy Bill at Independence Day Event
39.description: President Trump triumphantly highlighted tax cuts in the legislation while downplaying cuts to Medicaid and other assistance for poor Americans.
39.author: Chris Cameron

40.title: A bipartisan effort cuts unemployment benefits for high earners.
40.author: Talya Minsberg

41.title: Fear spreads in Texas, but hope as well.
41.description: Scenes from Central Texas and beyond captured the anxiety of those searching for loved ones after the Guadalupe River burst its banks.
41.author: Edgar Sandoval

42.title: Jewish Leaders Denounce Trump’s Use of Centuries-Old Trope
42.description: President Trump said he did not realize that the term “Shylock” was viewed as antisemitic.
42.author: Tyler Pager

43.title: Trump calls Zelensky after Russia hits Ukraine with a large air barrage.
43.description: It was the latest in a series of almost weekly large-scale missile and drone attacks. President Trump said he “didn’t make any progress” with President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia.
43.author: Andrew E. Kramer

44.title: How to Make the Biggest Splash, According to Science - WSJ
44.description: Cannonball, belly-flop or manu jumping? Physicists study the splashiest way to hit the water.
44.author: Nidhi Subbaraman
